message:
test(fcp-conformance/admission): pin AdmissionController back-pressure budget contract (br-d2uly)

AdmissionController is the per-peer back-pressure gate every connector
sits behind. 123 inline tests cover internals but no cross-crate
conformance pinned the contract. A regression that, e.g., zeroed
retry_after or merged per-peer state into a global counter would
silently break every connector's back-pressure handling.

Adds 9 tests against the public fcp_mesh::admission API:

- within_budget_admission_succeeds
- byte_budget_exceeded_returns_retry_after (the documented
  back-pressure signal — retry_after > 0; assertion verifies
  current + requested > limit at the rejection point)
- symbol_budget_exceeded_returns_retry_after
- authentication_required_short_circuits_before_budget_checks
  (cheap-reject ordering: AuthenticationRequired fires even on
  oversized requests, BEFORE budget cycles)
- authentication_required_passes_through_when_peer_is_authenticated
- per_peer_isolation_one_peer_overrun_does_not_affect_another
  (noisy-neighbour DoS defense)
- restrictive_preset_rejects_what_permissive_admits (4 MiB / 100
  symbols admitted under permissive caps; rejected under
  restrictive)
- budget_check_is_first_byte_then_symbol_when_both_would_exceed
  (deterministic error variant when multiple caps fail at once)
- fresh_peer_within_budget_does_not_count_against_others (per-peer
  keying; tracking is not aggregated globally)

All 9 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
admission_back_pressure_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
